hook_text: We rebuilt our leave engine three times. The third version is why clients don't leave us.
body:
Version 1: Simple accrual. Days earned per month, balance tracked.

It worked — until an employee worked a public holiday, took unpaid leave, and was on a fixed-term contract.

Three edge cases. Zero correct outputs.

Version 2: Rule-based with exceptions. We coded every Labour Law clause we could find.

It worked — until a client had employees across two Oman governorates with different local holiday calendars.

More edge cases. Partial failure.

Version 3: We stopped trying to anticipate edge cases.

We built a configurability layer — the admin sets the rules for their company, and the engine enforces whatever they configure, while hard-coding the non-negotiable Labour Law floors.

Result:
- Handles fixed-term, open-ended, and part-time contracts
- Supports company-specific holiday calendars
- Enforces Ministry minimums regardless of config
- Audit log of every rule change with timestamp

No client has churned because of leave calculation errors since Version 3.

---

The best software doesn't anticipate every edge case. It lets the customer define theirs.

What's the hardest edge case in your HR process?
rejected_reason: pre-PR#12 backlog purge — queue hygiene 2026-07-15 (regenerate via corrected pipeline)
